title: US withdraws American troops from Somalia as announced by Trump
justification: Article presents Trump administration troop withdrawal factually but uses selective framing emphasizing policy rationale while minimizing potential concerns; contains multiple redacted sections and incomplete quotes that obscure full context, and ends abruptly mid-sentence suggesting editorial incompleteness.

title: Fox Business Headline Reveals Mainstream Cluelessness About Inflation
justification: Moderate bias with selective framing and editorial commentary; the article uses charged language like 'mainstream cluelessness' and 'spinmeisters' to dismiss opposing interpretations, presents its economic analysis as obviously correct while dismissing mainstream media as confused, and emphasizes one perspective on inflation's relationship to retail sales without presenting genuine counterarguments.

title: Exclusive Video: Chaos Erupts in Texas Border Town as Los Zetas Cartel Attempts Complex Smuggling Operation
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing that emphasizes cartel coordination and control while presenting unverified claims about Los Zetas directing specific operations; the article lacks independent verification of the alleged 'complex smuggling operation' coordination and relies heavily on commentary from Brandon Darby without presenting counterarguments or skeptical perspectives.

title: Trump Slam Dunk: You're the "Big Man;" Biden Owes the American People an Explanation
justification: Strong bias evident through charged framing ('covering up,' 'dark money,' leading poll question), selective presentation favoring Trump while dismissing Democratic counterarguments as 'misinformation,' and a leading final question designed to imply misconduct without evidence.

title: Russian court orders seizure of billionaire oligarch's luxury hotel following criticism of Ukraine invasion
justification: The article relies heavily on Financial Times reporting and unnamed sources ('two people familiar with the matter,' 'person close to Deripaska') without independent verification; while the core claims about Deripaska's public statements and the court order appear plausible and traceable to credible sources, the framing emphasizing retaliation and the Kremlin's suppression creates selective emphasis favoring one narrative interpretation.

title: Benny Hinn RENOUNCES prosperity gospel, vows to never again ask for money
justification: Moderate bias: Article presents Hinn's renunciation as fact without verification, uses loaded language ('come to his senses,' 'finally coming to the truth'), applies subjective religious interpretation as objective fact, and frames expectations for his charity giving as a test of sincerity while omitting any counterargument or independent verification of the claimed renunciation.

title: Woman Allegedly Observed Performing Sex Act in NYC Street
justification: Strong bias evident through selective framing that attributes crime to Democratic policies and police defunding without evidence of causation, cherry-picked crime statistics presented without context, omission of alternative explanations, and inflammatory language ('shocking') designed to outrage readers.

title: Hamas Shoots Its Own Citizens and No One Seems to Care
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('terrorist government,' 'media shill,' 'doom Palestinians'), selective framing that exclusively portrays Hamas negatively while minimizing Israeli actions, and unsubstantiated claims about media blackouts and deliberate bias by major news organizations.

title: Elizabeth Warren: Biden's Infrastructure Budget 'Doesn't Quite Go Far Enough'
justification: Nearly neutral; article presents Warren's direct quote criticizing Biden's infrastructure budget with minimal editorial framing, though the selective headline emphasizes her criticism without context of broader Democratic support for the proposal.

title:
MATT TAIBBI ON THE TWITTER FILES:

These next few pieces are the result of looking at two discrete...
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis: the article presents a one-sided narrative about Twitter's relationship with government agencies using loaded language ('trained,' 'cede control,' 'involuntary contractor,' 'living the hell') that favors a critical stance toward government pressure without presenting counterarguments or the platform's perspective on content moderation rationale.

title: Just Stop! The NRA Hasn't 'Bought' Anyone
justification: Moderate bias with clear editorial stance favoring gun rights; selectively presents facts (2018 spending claim), dismisses opposing views as intellectually dishonest ('kafkatrap,' 'hubris of the anti-gunner'), uses charged language ('tired talking points,' 'annoying'), and omits counterarguments about campaign finance influence.

title: Portland police attacked in broad daylight after Antifa erects new 'Autonomous Zone'
justification: Strong bias through charged language ('brazenly threatening,' 'far Left activists,' 'Antifa'), selective framing that emphasizes police being 'beaten back' and attacked while omitting context about the eviction dispute, and inflammatory characterization of protesters' demands as 'free housing for all' rather than neutral description.

title: Failed Candidate Stacey Abrams Defends Using Identity Politics: 'It's Exactly How We Won'
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('Failed Candidate,' 'keeps insisting she somehow won,' 'funny how,' 'radical view'), selective facts omitting context, false equivalencies between Abrams' election integrity concerns and Trump's, and dismissive framing of her actual electoral organizing success in subsequent elections.

title: Ted Cruz goes completely bonkers
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy and inflammatory language: 'completely bonkers,' 'deranged,' 'weird creepy punchline,' 'creep,' 'whacked out cartoon villain,' and 'go back to whatever planet he came from' constitute personal attacks rather than analysis; selective presentation of Cruz's tweet without full context; characterization of his response as evidence of callousness without acknowledging potential satirical intent; omission of any substantive counterargument or defense of Cruz's position.

title: Biden tax plan would cost top 1% extra $160k per year... STEP Act will tax farmers, small business owners hardest.
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing, inflammatory language ("THIS IS MY SHOCKED FACE"), and editorializing that characterizes the plan as paying back unions and hitting small business owners hardest, while omitting substantive counterarguments or context about the policy's actual scope and rationale.

title: A Biden presidency would be devastating for unborn children around the world
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('unmitigated disaster,' 'brutal,' 'radical agenda'), selective presentation of facts, and one-sided framing that dismisses opposing views entirely while presenting speculative predictions as certainties.

title: House Impeachment Resolution Authorizes Fishing Expedition Probes that Go Far Beyond Ukraine
justification: Strong bias through charged language ('fishing expedition,' 'sham'), selective framing that emphasizes Democratic overreach while minimizing Ukraine allegations, one-sided presentation favoring Trump and Republicans, and omission of Democratic justifications for the broad probe scope.

title: Texas Land Commissioner Candidate Promises to Defund Schools Teaching 'Corrosive' Critical Race Theory
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing and charged language; phrases like 'race essentialist ideology,' 'radical left,' and 'hateful ideologies' present Avila's perspective as fact rather than opinion, while counterarguments from CRT supporters or school officials are entirely omitted.

title: VIDEO - Mike Lindell's Sends 10,000 MyPillows To Freedom Convoy In Canada
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('fake news industry', 'far left attacking'), selective framing that presents Lindell's claims without scrutiny, omission of context around election challenges, and inflammatory characterization of opposing views while presenting Lindell's narrative uncritically.

title: $1,000,000 in buried treasure hidden across state by jewelry store forced to close due to pandemic
justification: Nearly neutral reporting with minimal bias; the article presents Perri's treasure hunt business venture factually, though it uses slightly promotional language like 'tantalizing' and focuses primarily on the positive aspects of the story without exploring potential criticisms or skepticism.

title: Ron DeSantis Just Made a Bold Move Against the Biden DOJ's Overreaching Parental 'Threats' Directive
justification: Moderate bias through selective framing and charged language ('troubling directive,' 'overreaching,' 'sicking the Feds') that favors DeSantis's position while omitting the DOJ's stated rationale for the memo or counterarguments from education officials and school boards who supported federal involvement.

title: HARSH REBUKE: Liz Cheney slams Fox host blaming Capitol Police for Jan. 6
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('harsh rebuke,' 'shut down,' 'false narrative'), selective framing that presents only one perspective on disputed events, and inflammatory characterizations ('armed mob,' 'acts of treason and sedition') without presenting counterarguments or opposing viewpoints fairly.

title: Donald Trump: Investigate Adam Schiff for 'Fraud & Treason'
justification: Strong bias through selective quotation of Trump's statements without counterargument, inflammatory language ('sinister,' 'lying & cheating like never before'), and framing that presents only Trump's perspective on the Schiff controversy without including Schiff's defense, context about the impeachment process, or factual analysis of the disputed call transcript.

title: Liz Cheney Attacks Trump's White House in Opening Statement For Jan. 6 Hearing (VIDEO)
justification: Strong bias evident through charged language ('RINO,' 'ire'), selective facts omitting Cheney's actual investigative focus, unsubstantiated claims about FBI informants and Pelosi's responsibility presented as fact without evidence, and framing that dismisses the January 6 investigation while promoting alternative blame narratives.

title: Fox News Makes Excuses For Lara Logan
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('heinous,' 'toadie,' 'cover-up'), one-sided framing that dismisses opposing views, selective omission of context about Lara Logan's actual statements, and inflammatory characterizations without presenting the full scope of the debate.

title: Barack Obama's Presidential Library approves release of thousands of UFO-related files
justification: Article uses sensationalized framing ('jackpot,' 'tantalizing, enigmatic') and emphasizes mysterious aspects of UFOs while presenting the FOIA disclosure as more significant than warranted; selectively omits that most UFO cases have mundane explanations until the final sentence, and relies heavily on Greenewald's characterization rather than independent verification of claims.

title: VT Science: Debunking explosive properties of Ammonium Nitrate
justification: Extreme bias with fabricated claims: article falsely asserts the Beirut explosion was a nuclear weapon attack, misrepresents basic chemistry (claiming ammonium nitrate cannot explode), uses inflammatory language ('nuclear terror attack'), and makes unverifiable accusations against unnamed parties; contains conspiracy rhetoric and deceptive framing throughout.

title: Did the UK's Secret Libya Policy Contribute to the Manchester Terror Attack?
justification: Moderate bias with selective framing and omitted counterarguments; the article assumes a causal connection between UK Libya policy and the Manchester attack without presenting evidence or opposing perspectives, uses loaded language like 'establishment would rather avoid' and 'elephant in the room', and frames the inquiry as deliberately evasive without substantiation.

title: Facebook "Fact Checks" Woman Describing Her Own Symptoms from COVID-19 Vaccine Reaction - She Died One Week Later
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('scumbags,' 'maniacal,' 'experimental jabs,' 'poison'), misleading causal framing (implying vaccine caused death without evidence), selective fact presentation, and inflammatory rhetoric designed to promote vaccine distrust while omitting relevant medical context.

title: The LGBT community has become its own worst enemy
justification: Strong bias demonstrated through charged language ('sickening promotion', 'petulant children', 'reactionary hate-mobs'), selective framing that dismisses LGBT activism as irrational, and one-sided presentation that omits legitimate perspectives on representation and inclusion issues.

title: US Citizens Fund UN Arms Trade Treaty Which Impacts US Gun Ownership
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, inflammatory language (comparing UN to Hitler, claiming 'mind control techniques'), unsubstantiated conspiracy claims (Agenda 21/2030 as global control scheme), selective facts about UN funding, and dismissal of opposing views; uses charged rhetoric like 'oligarchy,' 'Big Brother,' and 'total control' without balanced presentation of counterarguments.

title: Jewish Leaders Tell Trump He Is Not Welcome In Pittsburgh
justification: Subtle bias shown through selective framing and emphasis: the article leads with and heavily emphasizes Jewish leaders' rejection of Trump while downplaying Trump's own statements and the complexity of the situation; word choices like 'blamed him for the massacre' and presenting the leaders' claims without sufficient counterbalance or Trump's defense of his rhetoric create a one-sided narrative.

title: House Democrats Forum: 'Extremists' Infiltrate Anti-Lockdown Protests to 'Terrorize'
justification: Moderate bias: the article uses framing that presents one perspective (extremists infiltrating protests) as established fact through panelists' assertions without meaningfully presenting counterarguments or skepticism about these claims; language like 'baselessly accusing' dismisses protesters' concerns, and the article selectively emphasizes extremist connections while omitting context about legitimate civil liberties debates during lockdowns.

title: MAGA Rep Matt Gaetz Torches 'McFailures' McConnell and McCarthy for Midterm Results
justification: Moderate bias shown through charged language ('flaccid performance,' 'McFailures'), selective emphasis on Gaetz's criticism while downplaying context, and framing that favors the MAGA perspective without adequately presenting counterarguments or broader context for the midterm results.

title: Michael Pento: 30-50% Market Drop From Here, Ushering In The Worst Bear Market Of Our Lifetime
justification: Moderate bias: The article presents Michael Pento's bearish market prediction as established fact ('will result in the worst bear market') without substantive counterarguments or alternative expert perspectives; framing emphasizes doom scenarios while omitting balanced analysis.

title: BREAKING: U.S. Senate Deals Democrats' Elections Takeover Bill a Critical Blow in Evenly Split Vote
justification: Strong bias through charged language ('radical Democrat media,' 'extremist Biden agenda,' 'left-wing media has been egregiously lying'), selective framing that dismisses voting rights concerns as false advertising, cherry-picked statistics about voter ID possession without context, and intentional mischaracterization of the For the People Act's purposes.

title: National Enquirer Had a Safe with a Treasure Trove of Dirt on Trump that It Kept Hidden During the C
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis; the article emphasizes damaging information about Trump while relying heavily on anonymized sources and unverified claims about the safe's contents, with minimal counterargument or alternative perspective presented.

title: New York Dems set to defy SCOTUS with new concealed carry laws
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('ram through,' 'legislative nightmare,' 'blatantly disregarding'), selective framing that portrays Democratic actions as hostile to Second Amendment rights, and dismissive treatment of gun control arguments without substantive counterarguments.

title: Cardiovascular Damage & Health
justification: Moderate bias: The article selectively presents peer-reviewed claims about supplements without acknowledging scientific debate or limitations, uses speculative framing about vaccine/COVID damage causation, and relies on anecdotal conversation with named doctors rather than rigorous evidence, while omitting counterarguments or critical analysis from mainstream medical organizations.

title: Feminists Must Not Shrink Away from the Arena of Ideas
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing that favors open debate over what the author characterizes as restrictive feminist practices; the article dismisses 'safe spaces' and 'no platforming' as intellectually detrimental without engaging substantive counterarguments about why marginalized groups employ these strategies.

title: Fraudulent Biden Admin BARS Nicaraguan President And Other Govt Officials From U.S. Citing ELECTION FRAUD
justification: Strong bias evident through charged language ('Fraudulent Biden Admin', 'laughing in your face'), false equivalence between U.S. and Nicaragua elections, selective framing that uses factual information about Nicaragua while editorializing about Biden, and prominent self-promotional sections that undermine journalistic neutrality.

title: Soleimani's 28 yo Daughter is an AMERICAN Citizen, Threatens Death to American Soldiers
justification: Strong bias with misleading framing, unverified claims presented as fact (Zeinab Soleimani being an American citizen), cherry-picked quotes taken out of context, inflammatory language ('reign of terror'), and selective omission of context while making sweeping accusations about Obama granting citizenship to Iranians without evidence.

title: Calls Mount for 'Criminal Investigation' Into Dr. Fauci's Crimes
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('crimes,' 'dishonesty,' 'perjury'), selective framing presenting unproven allegations as established fact, heavy reliance on speculation and opinion from Tucker Carlson rather than verified reporting, and misleading characterization of email contents without providing full context or allowing for alternative explanations.

title: Palmetto State Armory Goes Big With Latest Location
justification: Article contains factual reporting about PSA's expansion with specific details (69,000 sq ft, $2.65M acquisition, 20 firing lanes), but includes editorial commentary and opinion framing (e.g., 'as I suspect it will be,' suggestion to convert the Newseum, criticism of D.C. leadership) that subtly favors the gun company and Second Amendment advocacy over neutral reporting.

title: Aftermath of an Afghanistan debacle
justification: Moderate bias: The article uses loaded language ('crashed and burned,' 'debacle,' 'cynics might say') and selectively emphasizes American failure while omitting counterarguments about the complexity of nation-building or Taliban military superiority; presents opinions as facts and relies on dramatic framing rather than balanced analysis of the Afghanistan withdrawal.

title: Seattle Mayor Just Got Hit With 4th Teen-Sex Accusation
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('firestorm,' 'ambush'), selective framing emphasizing accusers' criminal histories while dismissing their relevance, partisan attacks on 'liberals,' and the opening paragraph's editorializing about political consequences rather than neutral reporting of facts.

title: America's "War on Terrorism," The Global Economic Crisis, Towards a World War III Scenario
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, inflammatory language ('web of deceit,' 'smokescreen,' 'military-intelligence ploy,' 'fabrication'), unfounded conspiracy claims about 9/11, and selective framing that dismisses mainstream perspectives without presenting counterarguments or evidence.

title: IT'S HAPPENING: Trump Team Begins Forensic Examination of Dominion Machines in Michigan
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing, inflammatory language ('army of white hats'), emphasis on allegations without adequate context, and characterization of election officials' explanations as suspect ('unexplained and so-called glitch') while omitting substantive counterarguments.

title: [WILFORD BRIMLEY VOICE] MR. TRUMP, ARE YOU THAT SMART? NEVER MIND, YOU AIN'T GONNA TELL ME THAT, AR...
justification: Strong bias demonstrated through selective presentation of facts, charged language ('complete rout,' 'it is to laugh'), unsupported speculation presented as fact ('appears to have been a setup'), omission of counterarguments, and one-sided advocacy for Trump while dismissing critics.

title: The annual atmospheric CO2 variation: A new theory - 'Could we be wrong about the annual cycle of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere?'
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing: the article presents an alternative scientific hypothesis as potentially revolutionary ('could change our understanding') without adequately contextualizing the mainstream scientific consensus on CO2 seasonal cycles, omits discussion of why the biological explanation is widely accepted, and emphasizes correlations without addressing causation or peer review status of the Oxford researchers' work.

title: Here are 4 Ways the Data You Create While Using the Internet Can Actually Be Used to Discriminate Ag
justification: Article is nearly neutral with objective language discussing how big data can be used discriminatorily; uses concrete examples but word choice like 'rammed through' is absent, maintaining factual tone throughout despite advocacy-leaning topic selection.

title: Trump Administration Hit With Legal News After Bullying NOAA
justification: Strong bias evident through charged language ('bullying,' 'fragile-ego'ed'), selective framing that presents Trump's actions negatively without counterargument, rhetorical questions that imply malfeasance ('How far are they willing to go'), and editorializing ('demonstrably false,' 'demonstrably wrong') that goes beyond neutral reporting.

title: SHOCKING: Philly FED Admits Biden's Bureau of Labor Statistics Overstated US Job Growth in 2022 by At Least 1.1 Million
justification: Strong bias with misleading framing: uses charged language ('SHOCKING,' 'overstated,' 'gamed and manipulated'), cherry-picks a Philadelphia Fed preliminary benchmark estimate as definitive proof of intentional BLS malfeasance, implies election-year conspiracy without evidence, omits that benchmark revisions are routine and often correct in both directions, and presents speculative interpretation as fact.

title: Cheney: 'No Question' Another Attack Like Jan. 6 Or Worse, Can Occur Due to Election Fraud Claims
justification: The article presents a selective quotation from Liz Cheney with multiple redacted sections marked as copyright, creating an incomplete and potentially misleading representation of her full statements; while the core claim is verifiable (Cheney did appear on ABC's This Week), the heavy redactions prevent full context assessment and the framing emphasizes her warnings without balancing counterarguments about election security concerns.

title: Biden asks federal workers to get vaccinated or face repeated COVID tests
justification: Nearly neutral reporting with minimal bias; factual presentation of Biden's policy announcement with balanced inclusion of both support (quotes from Biden, examples of vaccine mandates) and opposing views (Republican disagreement mentioned), though Republican counterarguments receive less detailed coverage than the policy itself.

title: Soros-Funded L.A. District Attorney Drops Death Penalty for 'Proud' Cop Killer
justification: Strong bias through inflammatory language ('Soros-Funded,' 'billionaire globalist,' 'whopping $1.5 million'), selective framing that emphasizes the defendant's statements while presenting the DA's decision negatively without substantive counterargument, and leading characterization of the DA's prosecutorial decision as wrongful without balanced legal context.

title: THE BONFIRE OF THE VANITY FAIR: Emo, Childish Beto Supporters Think He Got 'Punitive' Media Coverage...
justification: Strong bias evidenced by inflammatory language ('emo, childish'), dismissive framing of opposing views, selective quoting (with unexplained copyright symbols suggesting redaction), and charged editorializing that advocates against the subject while omitting substantive counterarguments.

title: Police: Rideshare Passenger Committed Suicide in Car on U. of Maryland Campus
justification: Article contains factual reporting on the incident but includes irrelevant tangential information (November shooting, unrelated Professor Blake quote about Electoral College) that disrupts coherence and suggests selective framing; some passages are garbled or incomplete, undermining clarity.

title: Mattis Says He Will Deploy US Military Forces to Protect 2018 Elections
justification: Moderate bias shown through charged language ('habitual liar,' 'dictatorial powers,' 'always lie'), selective framing that emphasizes Mattis's agreement with intelligence agencies while dismissing Trump's position, and the incomplete final sentence suggesting editorial cuts rather than substantive reporting.

title: ACTION ALERT Austrias top Jewish group tries to censor Black Panther leader
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, inflammatory language, and selective facts: uses dehumanizing terms like 'freedom-crushing culprits,' makes sweeping accusations about 'Zionists' controlling academia and 9/11 conspiracies, presents one-sided narrative defending the lecture series while dismissing opposing concerns, and calls for ejecting 'all Zionists' from academia.

title: Migrants Try to Buy Children to Illegally Enter U.S.
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('lefties,' 'weeping,' 'cash-grab'), selective framing that blames political opponents rather than presenting balanced analysis, dismissive tone toward critics ('as the media likes to call it'), and inflammatory editorial voice throughout that advocates rather than reports.

title: Watch A Cracking Up Trump Break Down And Repeat The Same Answer Over And Over On N. Korea
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy and charged language: 'mentally unstable,' 'mentally ill lunatic,' 'deranged tweet threats,' 'crazed buffoon,' and 'mentally fragile man' are inflammatory personal attacks rather than objective reporting; selective framing emphasizing repetitive answers while omitting context; editorializing throughout with interpretations presented as fact ('sounded like,' 'painted himself into a corner').

title: Trump Shows No Sympathy And Insults The Widow Of A Green Beret Killed In Niger Ambush
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('s**t happens' characterization, 'empty shell of a man'), and one-sided framing that dismisses Trump's statement as purely insulting while omitting any context or charitable interpretation; uses inflammatory descriptors ('incapable of empathy') presented as fact rather than opinion.

title: As Carlson's Advertisers Are Targeted, He Refuses to Apologize
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective use of inflammatory quotes, charged language ('shocking,' 'demented'), and emphasis on Carlson's refusal to apologize while omitting his stated rationale or full context of his explanations; the article frames the narrative heavily against Carlson without balanced counterargument.

title: Anti-Christian Media Slam Brees for Appearance in 'Anti-LGBT' Focus on the Family Video
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('swarmed over Brees like angry bees,' 'smear,' 'anti-Christian media'), and selective framing that dismisses criticism of Focus on the Family while characterizing opposing viewpoints as unfounded 'lies' and 'libel,' with facts presented to favor one ideological perspective.

title: IEA World Energy Outlook 2022
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing, charged language ('ludicrously named', 'fairy tale'), dismissive treatment of APS scenario, and emphasis on failure of climate agreements while omitting counterarguments about the complexity of global climate policy coordination.

title: Russian Central Bank Eyes Gold-Backed Crypto
justification: The article contains subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis that favors cryptocurrencies and the Bank of Russia's initiatives, while characterizing criticism as 'unmerited' and using charged language like 'a big shot in the arm for an industry.' Multiple copyright symbols obscure content, making full assessment difficult, but the visible portions show selective presentation favoring pro-crypto perspectives.

title: UPDATE: 71 out of 75 Districts in Uttar Pradesh, India - Its Most Populated State - Reported No Covid-19 Cases in 24 Hours After Implementing Ivermectin Protocol
justification: Strong bias with selective facts and misleading framing: the article cherry-picks data about Uttar Pradesh's ivermectin use while omitting crucial context (vaccination rates, testing patterns, natural disease cycles), uses loaded language ('media won't mention'), makes unsupported causal claims linking ivermectin to case reductions, and presents rhetorical questions ('what could the United States be doing wrong?') that advocate a particular viewpoint rather than presenting balanced analysis.

title: VIDEO: Detroit DoorDash Driver Saves Toddler Roaming Streets in Frigid Temperatures
justification: Article is primarily factual and neutral in reporting a rescue incident, with minimal editorial framing; however, the selective placement of the rescue narrative and lack of critical context about parental supervision create a very subtle lean toward portraying the driver positively without deeper examination.

title: Framing Narratives & Inducing Compliance Using Moral Foundations Theory
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('tyrannical dictates,' 'unconstitutional actions'), conspiratorial framing about elite manipulation, selective citation of sources to support predetermined conclusions about media deception, and dismissive characterization of public health measures without presenting counterarguments or evidence.

title: Wuxi Covid outbreak: Shopping for more than 2 hours, your ID card will be confiscated
justification: Moderate bias with selective framing and unverified claims presented as fact; the article emphasizes dramatic government overreach (ID card confiscation) without clearly distinguishing between official policy and disputed/denied claims, uses emotionally charged language like 'shocked the citizens,' and contains numerous redacted sections undermining transparency and verification.

title: Kellyanne Conway resorts to bizarre antics at Donald Trumps fake celebration
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, dehumanizing language (calling Trump a 'racist traitor' and 'fake president'), intentional mischaracterization of facts (claiming no Eagles players took a knee when the article itself acknowledges this happened), and cherry-picked anecdotes presented as evidence of wrongdoing while omitting the actual context of the Eagles' participation decision.

title: 'Saving the Dark' (Light pollution fanaticism)
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing that advocates for dark sky preservation while dismissing opposing viewpoints as 'fanaticism'; uses charged language like 'Deep Ecologists just don't like human beings' and presents one-sided arguments about light pollution without substantial counterarguments.

title: Mitch McConnell is Moving Forward on Impeachment Whether Democrats Like It Or Not
justification: Strong bias through charged language ('damage,' 'flounder'), selective framing that favors McConnell's position, dismissive characterization of Democratic demands, and omission of substantive counterarguments or context for why Democrats might withhold articles.

title: Trump Gives Out Personal Cell Number to World Leaders
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy language ('stuffed suits,' 'Liberals simply cannot stand,' 'media's hysteria'), selective framing that portrays Trump's action as routine while dismissing legitimate protocol concerns, and misleading comparisons to past presidents that omit crucial security context.

title: Exclusive - Sen. Kelly Loeffler: Church Camp's 'Disgusting' Child Abuse Allegation Should Disqualify Warnock
justification: Strong bias with selective framing, charged language ('disgusting,' 'should be disqualify'), and emphasis on allegations while omitting Warnock's explanations and the fact that no abuse reports were publicly confirmed; one-sided presentation of Loeffler's attack without substantive rebuttal.

title: EMPTY POCKETS: Sen. Rick Scott faces tough questions about GOP Senate campaign finances
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('in hot water,' 'mysteriously missing,' 'getting crushed'), selective framing that emphasizes wasteful spending while omitting NRSC's explanations, and inflammatory characterization of Scott's agenda ('destroying Social Security and Medicare') presented as fact rather than political framing.

title: Here's Why the Fed Hasn't Yet Invoked Its 13 (3) Emergency Powers to Stem a Stock Market Crash
justification: Strong bias through selective framing and charged language ('death spiral,' 'cheery news,' 'fairytales') presenting financial events with editorial commentary; the article emphasizes worst-case scenarios and uses sarcasm to favor a particular interpretive stance on Fed policy, while the title's premise about Section 13(3) powers is asserted without substantive analysis of why they haven't been invoked.

title: Urgent! Help Block the Confirmation of Tom Vilsack for Ag Secretary
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('failed miserably,' 'turned a blind eye'), selective omission of Vilsack's accomplishments, and framing designed to motivate opposition rather than inform; specific claims about USDA enforcement failures lack detailed evidence or counterargument.

title: Tucker Carlson's insipid accusation of anti-Trump "collusion" by The New York Times
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('hoodwink,' 'hate campaign,' 'insipid'), selective framing that presents one interpretation of events as fact, dismissive characterization of opposing views, and omission of Carlson's potential counterarguments or context for his statements.

title: Tim Tebow's Engagement Draws Insulting Media Responses
justification: Moderate bias: The article uses selective framing and charged language to present media criticism as unfair, characterizing legitimate editorial commentary as 'insulting' and 'classless,' while presenting Tebow's engagement through an idealized lens and comparing criticism of his personal choices to dismissal of abuse perpetrators, creating a false equivalence.

title: Watch Joe Biden in 2005 and 1987 Destroy Joe Biden Today on Court-Packing
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('corrupt power grab,' 'throw her thoughts under the bus,' 'politicize'), selective presentation of Biden quotes without full context, and dismissive framing of Biden's cognitive abilities ('How much he has deteriorated since then is really shocking').

title: Economic Decay Leads to Social and Political Decay
justification: Moderate bias with clear editorial stance: uses charged language ('Gulag,' 'perverse system,' 'rot'), selectively presents drug policy arguments without counterarguments, makes sweeping claims about reform ineffectiveness, and advocates strongly for specific structural changes while dismissing alternative solutions as mere 'virtue-signaling.'

title: Acting Defense Secretary Chris Miller Thanks Airmen Who Supported Nigerian Hostage Rescue
justification: Nearly neutral reporting with minimal bias; straightforward account of an official visit and rescue operation details, though some quotes and framing subtly emphasize positive aspects without substantial counterbalance or critical perspective.

title: Jack Maxey Delivered Copy Of Hunter Biden's Laptop To Sen. Grassley, Pleaded For Investigation In Letter- Read It Here
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('champion of the truth,' 'sole power keeping,' 'terrify'), selective presentation of facts that assumes criminality without evidence, dismissal of mainstream media as fearful/corrupt, and framing that advocates for a specific investigation while omitting counterarguments or context about the laptop's origins and verification disputes.

title: The High-Pressure Business Of Selling 'Woke Protection' To Corporations
justification: Strong bias through charged language ('woke protection,' 'exploiting cancel culture,' 'Marxist-inspired'), selective framing that presents progressive initiatives negatively while omitting substantive counterarguments, and emphasis on criticism of left-wing groups without balanced presentation of their stated rationale or impact.

title: Ocasio-Cortez Supporters Love Socialism So Much That They Have Absolutely No Idea How It Works
justification: Strong bias through selective framing, loaded language ('mythical utopian,' 'government freebies,' 'clueless'), and dismissive characterization of supporters; presents a one-sided video compilation as evidence while omitting substantive counterarguments about socialist policies or funding mechanisms.

title: Showdown: Donald Trump Responds to 'Madman' Kim Jong-Un
justification: Moderate bias: The article uses charged language like 'madman' and 'dotard,' selectively quotes inflammatory statements from both sides without providing balanced context or counterarguments, and frames the conflict in antagonistic terms that favor a particular editorial perspective.

title: China to implement PERMANENT ban on wildlife trade in the wake of coronavirus outbreak
justification: The article presents factual events (China's wildlife trade ban, coronavirus outbreak details) but uses subtle framing that emphasizes China's accountability and 'shortcomings' while omitting context about wildlife trade's role in disease transmission globally, and contains unexplained copyright symbols suggesting editorial redaction or incomplete sourcing.

title: CDC Reports the Total Presumed Deaths Related to Coronavirus At 38,000 - Deaths Peaked in Early April
justification: Strong bias demonstrated through inflammatory language ('to hell with the average American'), selective presentation of data, unsubstantiated conspiracy claims (suggesting deliberate inflation of death counts for political purposes), and dismissive framing of CDC reporting while promoting alternative sources without proper context.

title: Would-Be Reagan Assassin John Hinckley Books NYC Concert
justification: The article reports verifiable facts about John Hinckley's 1981 assassination attempt, his music career, and his scheduled release, but employs mildly loaded language such as 'would-be assassin' and 'failed assassin' (rather than neutral descriptors), and selectively emphasizes sensational elements of his background while presenting his music aspirations without critical perspective.

title: Gov. Abbott Sues Biden Administration Over National Guard Vaccine Mandate
justification: The article uses selective framing and emphasis that favors Abbott's legal position, focusing heavily on his authority claims while minimizing counterarguments; the phrase 'will not tolerate' is quoted language that conveys strong opposition, and the explanation of legal ambiguity presented as potentially supporting Abbott's case shows subtle bias in how the constitutional questions are characterized.

title: PolitiFact Helper: Tim Ryan 'Mostly True,' J.D. Vance 'Mostly False' in Ohio Senate Race
justification: Strong bias with selective framing and cherry-picked statistics; the article uses charged language ('finesse,' 'mudslinging,' 'Play-Doh') and presents a one-sided accusation of partisan bias at PolitiFact without providing PolitiFact's full reasoning or allowing them to respond to the allegations.

title: Chart: Debt-to-GDP Continues to Rise Around the World
justification: Nearly neutral article presenting factual economic data on global debt-to-GDP ratios with minimal editorial bias; language is objective and sources are cited, though the opening framing slightly emphasizes pandemic pessimism.

title: The USDA is Encouraging People to Register Their Garden Under the Guise of a Community Program Called "The People's Garden"
justification: Strong bias through inflammatory framing ('under the guise of,' 'They're literally creating a map,' 'Guess what that map will be used for later?') that presents a legitimate USDA community program as surveillance with sinister intent, while omitting the actual stated purposes and benefits of the program.

title: Are The Democrats Really in a Bear Market?
justification: Strong bias with inflammatory language ('traitors,' 'rig elections,' 'communist countries'), unsubstantiated claims ('Democrats are able to rig elections and get away with it'), selective cherry-picked facts, and charged partisan rhetoric throughout that advocates for one political perspective while dismissing the opposing side.

title: Patrick Lancaster went into Avastol Steel Plant Territory
justification: Moderate bias: Article uses selective framing emphasizing Russian/DPR military perspective, presents claims about ceasefire and civilian releases without corroborating sources, omits Ukrainian perspectives, and uses language like 'life is coming back' that suggests normalization of occupation rather than balanced conflict reporting.

title: Italy: the "Legal Confederation for Human Rights" Demands a Moratorium on Vaccination Mandate
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('freedom-killing measures', 'vaccination blackmail', 'very brutal'), selective presentation of facts (emphasizing vaccine safety concerns while omitting efficacy data), misleading framing of legal rulings, and one-sided advocacy against vaccination mandates without meaningful counterargument.

title: Marjorie Taylor Greene just handed us a gift
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('gift', 'disrespecting'), selective framing of Greene's statements without direct quotes or context, and explicit calls for political action against Republicans; presents opinion as fact while omitting counterarguments.

title: KY Governor Blames Zombie Television Shows For Mass Shootings
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing and editorial commentary; the article presents Governor Bevin's statements but frames them dismissively ('grasping at straws,' 'completely out of touch'), omits his full context or any supportive perspectives on media violence concerns, and uses sarcastic rhetorical questions to undermine his position rather than neutrally reporting the claim.

title: Biden: We'll 'Finish the Job of Getting a Total of $2,000' in Relief
justification: Nearly neutral reporting that directly quotes Biden's statement about coronavirus relief with minimal editorial framing; the article presents his proposal straightforwardly without charged language or omitted context.

title: Trump Campaign Sues Nevada Over New Mail-In Ballot Law Ahead
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing, charged language ('illegal late night coup' quoted without context), one-sided emphasis on Trump's claims without equal weight to counterarguments, and incomplete presentation of the law's actual provisions versus how they're characterized.

title: The Midterms Maine
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing, charged language ('toss granny off the cliff'), dismissive tone toward Democratic positions ('Um... what about state welfare programs there, Skippy?'), and emphasis on favorable polling for the Republican while omitting Democratic counterarguments or Golden's policy details.

title: Central Banks Claim to Preserve the Value of Money Through Inflation
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('try to fool us,' 'faulty economic theory') and selective presentation of central bank statements without counterarguments; presents one economic perspective (Austrian school) as fact while dismissing mainstream economic consensus.

title: PETA Debunks University of Washington's Lies About Primate Research, One by One
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('soul-crushing reality,' 'horrors,' 'desperate and profoundly distraught'), selective presentation of facts, and one-sided advocacy against the university without substantive counterarguments or acknowledgment of the research center's stated rationale.

title: FRUITS OF A DECADES-LONG PROPAGANDA CAMPAIGN: Disturbing number of students say hate speech is not ...
justification: Strong bias demonstrated through charged language ('Disturbing number,' 'propaganda campaign') that frames student views negatively without presenting evidence, context, or opposing perspectives; the incomplete/corrupted article text prevents full assessment but the headline itself contains overt advocacy against a particular viewpoint.

title: About Face Forward Live: New York Eliminates Mask Rule for ALL
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and emotional appeals; the article quotes a legitimate policy announcement but adds personal anecdotes and value judgments ('confusing and cruel') that favor one perspective on mask policies, while omitting public health rationales or opposing viewpoints.

title: Missouri Senate Candidates React to Allegations Against Greitens - in No Uncertain Terms
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing, charged language ('rammed through' replaced by editorial commentary), and emphasis on scandals while providing minimal context on other candidates' positions; the heavy use of copyright redactions obscures full analysis but visible portions show dismissive framing of opposing views.

title: Brits Told To Get A Vaccine If They Want Their freedoms Back
justification: The article uses selective framing and emphasis to highlight vaccine mandates as restrictive ('Harsh restrictions will remain in place longer if Britons refuse to be vaccinated') while presenting officials' statements without sufficient context or counterbalance; the headline is somewhat sensationalized ('Told To Get A Vaccine If They Want Their freedoms Back') compared to the nuanced statements actually quoted.

title: Biden Communications Team Asked Journalists For Questions In Advance According To Report
justification: Moderate bias with selective framing and editorializing; phrases like 'rightly ruffled the feathers' and 'unprecedented in modern politics' inject opinion, while the article emphasizes wrongdoing without balanced context and includes loaded rhetorical questions ('Or the Joe Biden administration?') that guide readers toward a predetermined conclusion.

title: Trump family troubles: Don Jr. and Lara Trump implode in their Sunday AM talk show appearances
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy against Trump, charged language ('shameless lies,' 'manufactured scandal,' 'peddling'), selective framing that presents Trump family statements as embarrassing without balanced counterargument, and dismissive characterization ('parody of the scion of a third world dictator'). The article advocates for voting against Trump rather than reporting neutrally.

title: Looking Back on 2022: Ukraine and the Rest of the Story
justification: Moderate bias evident through selective framing that emphasizes negative consequences of Western actions (arms sales, sanctions impact on Global South) while presenting Russia's invasion more neutrally; omits discussion of Ukraine's sovereignty concerns and agency; uses phrases like 'reckless threats' and 'huge boon to arms merchants' that favor an anti-Western perspective; implies Western focus on Ukraine reflects hegemonic interests rather than legitimate security concerns.

title: Twenty-One Charged for Alleged Coronavirus Fraud Totaling $150 Million
justification: Nearly neutral reporting on DOJ charges with factual information presented straightforwardly; minor bias appears in the selective inclusion of the White House minimization comment and the AP reporting on $100 billion losses, which frames government response somewhat negatively, though this remains subtle and the article maintains largely objective language and structure.

title: WATCH: Gun owner tells Beto "Hell no, you're not" taking my guns!
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('triggered,' 'LOL'), dismissive framing of Beto's position ('odd way,' 'logic is too sound to refute'), selective presentation of the exchange favoring one perspective, and editorializing commentary that ridicules rather than reports.

title: VIDEO Trump says he will recommend Russia be allowed back in G7
justification: Article presents Trump's statements factually with basic context about Russia's 2014 removal from G7/G8, but the framing emphasizes Trump's advocacy without substantive counterarguments or opposing perspectives on Russia's actions in Ukraine, showing subtle bias toward presenting his position.

title: Aaron Klein: Netanyahu's Legacy Confirms Israel as a Global Superpower
justification: Strong bias: Article presents one-sided advocacy for Netanyahu through his adviser's opinion piece, uses charged language like 'consequential world leader' and 'historic peace deals,' omits substantial counterarguments about Netanyahu's controversies (judicial reform, settlement expansion, Gaza conflict), and selectively emphasizes achievements while ignoring documented criticisms.

title: Monkey Werx: The Watchman Hour - Four Horsemen
justification: Strong bias through conspiratorial framing (references to 'Four Horsemen' and 'Watchman Hour' suggest apocalyptic narratives), unsubstantiated claim about Kamala Harris's racial identity being deliberately changed, selective content presentation, and editorial promotion of unverified claims without supporting evidence or counterargument.

title: Hooray for It: Trump's Campaign Received Some Phenomenal Funding From a Most Unlikely Place
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('dastardly devil,' 'bodaciously bad billionaire'), selective presentation of facts (emphasizes Trump's single large donation while downplaying the 11:1 ratio of Biden donors to Trump donors), and inflammatory framing that ridicules opponents while celebrating Trump.

title: Marcus Stroman Blasts Mets For 'Not Doing Enough' To Prevent Racist Comments
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('brash individual,' 'famous for causing trouble,' 'dragging the Mets down'), dismissive framing of racism concerns ('laughable,' 'somewhat ridiculous'), selective omission of Stroman's specific allegations, and strawman arguments that minimize legitimate complaints about organizational responsibility.

title: 3 Dead, 2 Wounded After Terrorist Attack On US Base In Kenya
justification: The article reports on a real 2020 attack at Manda Bay Airfield in Kenya with verifiable details (al-Shabaab attack, U.S. Africa Command confirmation, casualties), but uses sensationalized framing typical of the source, contains multiple copyright symbol artifacts suggesting poor text quality, and presents unverified claims (al-Shabaab's damage assertions) as fact without clear attribution or skepticism.

title: Illegal Alien in Michigan Charged with Killing 14-Year-Old Boy on Bike in Hit and Run VIDEO
justification: Strong bias evident through inflammatory language ('Separating Child from His Parents Forever through Murder' in headline), repetitive emphasis on immigration status, charged framing that advocates against the defendant, and selective presentation designed to inflame rather than inform; the copyrighted redactions prevent full assessment but visible portions show overt advocacy with emotionally manipulative phrasing.

title: Pelosi Has Two Words For Boebert And Greene
justification: Strong bias through dehumanizing language ('lowest specimens of skinbags'), charged inflammatory framing ('slither down the halls'), selective presentation of events to favor Pelosi, and advocacy disguised as reporting with the concluding praise ('smoothest badass grandma').

title: Susan Collins' Phony 4th Of July Message Goes Horribly Wrong
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('phony,' 'horribly wrong,' 'corrupt president,' 'enabled this crisis'), and selective presentation of facts that portrays Collins negatively while omitting her actual policy positions or counterarguments; uses loaded framing and assumes malicious intent.

title: Michael Moore: Trump Supporters Are 'Our Taliban'
justification: Strong bias: charged language ('religious nuts,' 'cash-grab'), selective presentation of Moore's tweets without full context, misleading juxtaposition equating Trump supporters to Taliban, and one-sided framing that emphasizes inflammatory statements while omitting counterarguments or Moore's intended meaning.

title: Georgia election official's testimony will refute Donald Trump's accusations that she stole votes
justification: Article presents factual information about Moss's testimony with slight favorable framing toward her perspective; however, the numerous copyright symbols obscuring text prevent full assessment of bias, and the accessible portions show balanced attribution of claims to named sources without inflammatory language.

title: TOMORROW: Wisconsin House Speaker Robin Vos Agrees to Election Integrity Meeting to Discuss Gableman Report and Decertification of Wisconsin's 2020 Election
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('fraudulent,' 'rigged election,' 'fake news media,' 'covering up'), unsubstantiated claims presented as fact (95-100% nursing home turnout without evidence, Zuckerberg funding claims), selective presentation favoring election integrity advocates, and dismissal of opposing views while portraying pressure tactics positively.

title: Trump Super PAC Hit With Federal Probe Over Potential Corruption
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing (emphasizing 'corruption' and 'scandal' in headline), omission of counterarguments or Trump PAC's perspective, and reliance on single-source characterization (The Daily Beast) without balanced presentation of the FEC's actual findings or the organizations' explanations.

title: Kamala Harris Is The Biggest Gift Biden Could Have Given To Trump
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('lowest of the low,' 'dodged a bullet'), selective facts, and misleading characterizations; presents opinion as fact while omitting substantive counterarguments and relying on inflammatory rhetoric rather than balanced analysis.

title: Brazil needs $10 bln a year in aid for carbon neutrality by 2050, minister says
justification: Nearly neutral reporting of a minister's statements about Brazil's carbon neutrality funding needs, with factual claims about deforestation trends and negotiation positions presented straightforwardly, though the article's fragmented nature and multiple copyright symbols suggest transmission issues rather than editorial bias.

title: CHANGE: USA Powerlifting bans all trans women from competing as women. I sense a growing pushback ...
justification: Moderate bias: the article presents a clear editorial stance against trans women competing in sports, uses leading questions and rhetorical framing ("hard to swallow," "erases any distinction") that favors one perspective, selectively emphasizes biological differences while dismissing counterarguments, and frames contemporary orthodoxy dismissively without presenting substantive opposing viewpoints.

title: BUSTED! Three Forgotten Videos of Hypocrites Nadler & Pelosi Arguing the Impropriety of Impeachment
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('Fat Jerry', 'bloviated', 'brain dead', 'hypocrite'), personal attacks unrelated to substance ('weight loss', 'on drugs'), selective use of old video clips without full context, and dismissive framing of opposing views while heavily editorializing against Nadler and Democrats.

title: 4 Things to Know About Biden's New Voting Czar
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing that emphasizes Levitt's inaction during his DOJ tenure while mischaracterizing Georgia's voting law; the article presents the Public Interest Legal Foundation's critical assessment prominently without balancing context, and the Georgia law correction at the end undermines Levitt's position but frames it as a factual error rather than exploring the substantive policy disagreement.

title: James Woods: 'Founding Fathers Wrote the Second Amendment for Violent Haters Like Chuck Schumer'
justification: Strong bias through selective framing, inflammatory language ('violent haters,' 'civil war territory'), and one-sided presentation that omits Schumer's full context or perspective; the article amplifies Woods' characterization without journalistic balance or counter-analysis.

title: Here's How Bernie Can Still Win (Russian Conspiracy Edition)
justification: Strong bias with misleading framing and cherry-picked facts; the headline uses sarcasm to mock Sanders while presenting Russian interference claims as fact, selectively quoting experts without counterargument, and the overall tone advocates against Sanders while presenting unverified causal claims between Russian messaging and Sanders' campaign viability.

title: Runaway Texas Democrat Roasted On Live TV By Fox News Host - Watch
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('roasted,' 'bragged,' 'dodged,' 'regurgitated'), selective framing that favors Hegseth's perspective, dismissive characterization ('teenage-looking 32-year-old'), and omission of substantive counterarguments to the voting legislation criticisms.

title: Amazon Sidewalk to Expand "Smart Home" Capabilities Up to ½ Mile into Neighborhoods - Privacy Be Damned
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy against smart home technology, including unsubstantiated claims about electromagnetic radiation causing illness and cancer, selective misrepresentation of Amazon Sidewalk's purpose and function, inflammatory framing ('Privacy Be Damned'), and dismissive language toward the technology without presenting Amazon's perspective or legitimate counterarguments.

title: WOOPS! Jeff Bezos just outed AMI and David Pecker for trying to EXTORT him while working with the DOJ!
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing, charged language ('WOOPS!', 'outed', 'ruined'), and editorial commentary ('You think maybe the Justice Department will have a few questions') that advocates for a particular interpretation while omitting AMI's or Pecker's perspective on the situation.

title: Graham Predicts Majority of GOP Senators Will Vote for Testimony From Corrupt Bidens
justification: Strong bias evident through charged language ('corrupt Bidens,' 'corrupt Ukrainian company'), selective presentation of Graham's statements without counterarguments, framing claims as established fact rather than allegations, and one-sided emphasis on accusations while dismissing Democratic responses as mere 'dismissal' rather than substantive rebuttals.

title: Another FCC Lawsuit Challenges Agency's Order Nixing Evaluation of Federal RF Radiation Limits
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('captured,' 'shenanigans,' 'unsafe'), selective presentation of facts favoring one perspective, and unsubstantiated claims about RF radiation safety while omitting FCC scientific rationale or mainstream scientific consensus; presents activist lawsuits as factual without balanced analysis.

title: Thank you Bernie Sanders!
justification: Subtle bias shown through selective framing that favors Bernie Sanders' actions while dismissing prior concerns; uses loaded language like 'embarrassing' and 'horrifying behavior' to characterize 2016 delegates, presents Sanders' motives charitably without scrutiny, and omits counterarguments about delegate conduct or party unity efforts.

title: "My Job Is to Make Syria a Quagmire for the Russians": CIA Doctrine Made Official Policy
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('disastrous,' 'war machine,' 'hawkish'), selective framing that presents US motives conspiratorially while treating Russian/Iranian presence as legitimate, omission of counterarguments, and inflammatory characterization of US policy as intentional deception rather than strategic disagreement.

title: NBA legend Charles Barkley says Dems & GOP allied in dividing and conquering America [Video]
justification: Subtle bias shown through selective framing and emphasis: the article prominently features Barkley's criticism of both parties but characterizes conservative response positively ('cheered') while describing liberal response negatively ('accused him'), and includes contextual details about his past Republican affiliation and police reform views that frame him sympathetically without balanced counterarguments.

title: COVID's Painful Side Effect: Deep Budget Cuts At State And Local Levels
justification: Nearly neutral reporting on pandemic-related budget cuts with factual claims about layoffs, state warnings, and survey data; minimal editorial framing though the opening emphasizes severity ('no precedent in modern times') and some sections are slightly selective in focus.

title: The newest redefinition of traditional marriage: 'platonic marriages'
justification: Strong bias through charged language ('hollowed out,' 'gutted,' 'fiercely denied,' 'coldblooded contract'), selective framing that presents religious traditionalist views as objective fact while dismissing other perspectives, and editorial commentary disguised as reporting that advocates against same-sex marriage and alternative relationship structures.

title: 'Chilling!' 'Vile!' 'Vicious!' WashPost Wails After McSally Calls CNN Reporter, 'Liberal Hack'
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('media meltdown,' 'thinnest skin,' 'absurd'), selective framing that dismisses legitimate criticism as oversensitive, false equivalencies (comparing Democratic fundraising to the incident), and repeated use of scare quotes to delegitimize opposing viewpoints while defending McSally's conduct.

title: BBC Preps Emergency Radio Scripts For Possible Winter Energy Shortages, Blackouts
justification: Nearly neutral reporting with minimal bias; relies on Guardian reporting and presents BBC's emergency planning as routine procedure, though framing 'secretly prepared' slightly emphasizes secrecy without editorial comment.

title: Hot Mic Fool Crookie: Larry Kudlow
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('fool,' 'snarling,' 'right wingers decided they'd rather die'), selective framing that presents only one perspective on vaccine distribution while dismissing opposing views, and inflammatory characterization of political opponents.

title: President Trump Gives EO to Jump Start Commercial Drone Deliveries
justification: Article exhibits subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis: presents drone delivery benefits prominently while minimizing regulatory concerns, uses positive language for Trump's initiative ('jump start,' 'move faster, fly higher'), and heavily emphasizes cost advantages of drone delivery without balanced discussion of safety or privacy concerns; however, does include some factual regulatory context.

title: Who Wants Old-School Ed? State University Seeks a 'Director of Social Justice Education'
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('ultra-woke,' 'wokeness'), dismissive framing of social justice education, selective omission of the position's legitimate educational context, and sarcastic rhetorical questions that guide readers toward a predetermined conclusion rather than presenting balanced analysis.

title: GOP Rep. Andy Biggs Calls for Impeachment of DHS Secretary Mayorkas - Biden's Open Borders Czar
justification: Strong bias evident through charged language ('open borders czar,' 'Biden Effect,' 'reckless abandonment'), selective framing that emphasizes only one perspective on border policy, and omission of counterarguments or context about apprehension statistics and their interpretation.

title: Remember Afghanistan Trumps Strategy is Failing Badly as Taliban Gains Ground
justification: The article uses selective framing and emotional language like 'harsh questions' and 'serious military setbacks' while emphasizing Taliban gains and Afghan casualties, with limited counterargument or context about U.S. strategy effectiveness; multiple copyright symbols obscure specific details that would aid verification.

title: Maher: The Pandemic Is 'Over' and How We Handle Emerging from It 'Looking Like a Big Liability' for Democrats
justification: The article presents Maher's commentary with selective framing that emphasizes criticism of Democratic pandemic responses; while quotes appear authentic, the article omits context about public health guidance, relies heavily on one commentator's opinion without counterargument, and uses Maher's charged language (calling mask-wearers 'morons') without editorial distance.

title: How the FBI Tamed Muckraking Reporter Ned Day
justification: Moderate bias with selective narrative framing: the article presents an unverified anecdotal account as fact, uses charged language ('tamed,' 'conditioned'), omits corroborating evidence for key claims about FBI coercion and car bombing, and relies heavily on a single quoted source without journalistic verification or opposing perspective.

title: Money Spent By Both Parties in 2020 Election Could've Nearly Ended Homelessness In US
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('child sniffer,' 'p***y grabber,' 'tyrannical governors'), selective fact presentation favoring a cynical anti-establishment narrative, inflammatory characterizations of both candidates as 'worst in US history,' and misleading framing that omits context about election spending necessity and homelessness causation.

title: Climate Change alarmists Chrissy Teigen and John Legend have a fantastically hypocritical Valentine's day
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('mind-numbingly stupid,' 'onanistic praise,' 'crass pathetic hypocrite'), selective framing that presents the private jet incident as definitive hypocrisy without allowing for context or counterargument, and dismissive treatment of climate concerns as 'alarmism' and 'holocaust'; the article assumes bad faith motivation throughout.

title: Paid CNN Employee Endorses Joe Biden On-Air, Urges Bernie Bros to Join Him
justification: Moderate bias through selective framing and loaded language: the headline characterizes Yang's endorsement as problematic by emphasizing he was a 'paid CNN employee,' the article focuses heavily on Yang's appeals to Bernie supporters with repeated Cooper prompts to make the case against Sanders voters, and the framing suggests Yang was improperly influenced (Biden 'called' him, 'hard to say no'), while omitting context about the legitimacy of on-air endorsements by political commentators.

title: Steve Kerr Says Objecting to Kneeling During the National Anthem is 'Race-Baiting'
justification: Moderate bias: The article uses loaded language ('race-baiting,' 'slammed'), selectively presents Kerr's statements, and includes a whataboutism argument about China to undermine his credibility without allowing for response or context.

title: Heres how Trump will avoid the protests during his London trip
justification: Moderate bias: The article uses dismissive language ('stupid baby Trump balloon,' 'dumb baby balloon'), selectively presents facts to suggest Trump is avoiding protests despite official denials, and editorializes by speculating whether officials are 'telling the whole truth' while expressing personal satisfaction with the arrangement.

title: Leaked document suggests Russia is anticipating a 'massive medical emergency' due to Ukraine war casualties: report
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis: the article relies heavily on a single leaked document without independent verification, presents Ukrainian military claims without counterbalance, uses charged language like 'massive medical emergency' and 'huge losses,' and omits Russian perspective or explanation.

title: Chile Moves to Protect People's Minds Against Technology with Laws
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and rhetorical questions that favor the reform perspective; opens with loaded question about Facebook, uses sci-fi comparisons to amplify concern, and emphasizes potential harms while minimizing discussion of beneficial applications and regulatory balance.

title: Putin Rejects Gay 'Marriage', Considers Amending Constitution to Protect Traditional Marriage
justification: Article shows subtle bias through selective framing and omission: it emphasizes Putin's traditional values alignment without presenting counterarguments or LGBTQ+ perspectives, uses the phrase 'liberal Western values' which carries editorial connotation, and focuses heavily on Putin's opposition while omitting any discussion of LGBTQ+ rights advocacy or international responses.

title: The disturbing link between vaccines and pediatric cancer
justification: Strong bias with selective misrepresentation of FDA guidance: the article cherry-picks language about theoretical viral contamination risks from vaccine manufacturing to falsely imply vaccines cause pediatric cancer, while omitting that the FDA statement discusses potential risks that are monitored and that no causal link between vaccines and childhood cancer has been established. The framing deliberately conflates regulatory acknowledgment of theoretical manufacturing considerations with actual cancer causation.

title: Social Distancing: Just Do It!
justification: Nearly neutral public health guidance on social distancing with standard COVID-19 information; minor editorial framing in phrases like 'clear and present danger' and 'hardest safety rule' shows subtle preference for emphasizing risk, but overall conveys factual health recommendations without significant bias.

title: Poll: Majority of GOP Voters Think Donald Trump Should Start a New Party
justification: Nearly neutral reporting of a Rasmussen Reports poll with verifiable details (poll date, sample size, percentages); slight framing bias in leading with GOP majority support and emphasizing Trump's consideration of a third party, but includes counterstatements from RNC Chair McDaniel and Trump adviser Miller.

title: Why Gun Control Doesn't End School Shootings in US
justification: Strong bias with selective framing, charged language ('using the tragedy as a vehicle,' 'Dems aren't looking for solutions'), omission of Democratic counterarguments, mischaracterization of motives, and inflammatory comparisons (Nazi Germany analogy); multiple copyright symbols obscure content but don't negate the article's clear advocacy against gun control.

title: Kevin McCarthy Draws Battle Line Against Democrats: Releases Plans to Force Tough Votes
justification: Article uses charged language ('leftist agenda,' 'socialist agenda,' 'off the hook') and frames Republican strategy in adversarial terms while presenting McCarthy's perspective as primary; however, it remains factually grounded in describing actual procedural tactics and includes specific examples of Democratic defections, though counterarguments about Democratic policy rationales are largely omitted.

title: JUST IN: Senator Hawley asks DOJ to investigate St. Louis prosecutor who seized McCloskeys guns
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('corrupt,' 'unacceptable abuse of power,' 'politically motivated'), one-sided framing that presents only Hawley's perspective as fact without counterargument, selective omission of context (no mention of Gardner's statements or legal basis for seizure), and characterization of prosecutors' actions as malicious rather than presenting both perspectives objectively.

title: "He's Still Sleeping": Elon Musk Mocks President Biden for Utter Incompetence
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('missing in action,' 'America crumbles,' 'President Joe Biden thinks it is a perfect time to relax'), selective framing that presents Musk's criticism without counterargument, and inflammatory assertions about Biden's fitness that lack substantiation.

title: Actor Bette Midler regrets making fun of First Lady Melania Trump's accent and calling her an illegal immigrant
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('hate speech', 'offensive', 'racial attack'), selective and incomplete quotations with copyright symbols replacing content, inflammatory framing that presents Midler's tweets as worse than they may have been, and unverifiable specific claims about 'more than 1,600 followers' and details about Streisand's accusations that lack proper sourcing.

title: The Best Way To Fight Crime Doesn't Involve Gun Control
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing that presents gun control groups as contradicting themselves, rhetorical questions that guide readers toward the author's conclusion, and emphasis on one perspective while dismissing opposing views as ineffective without substantive counterargument.

title: New Zealand Proposes a Tax on Farts
justification: Subtle bias through heavy use of puns and mockery ('stinks,' 'blowing it,' 'dutch oven,' 'bull--') that trivializes the policy while maintaining factual reporting; framing emphasizes absurdity rather than presenting balanced analysis of the environmental or economic rationale.

title: Italy's Matteo Salvini Slams Chinese Communists over Hong Kong
justification: The article presents Salvini's criticism of China with selective framing that favors his perspective; while factual events are reported, the structure emphasizes his condemnation while relegating the Chinese Embassy's counterarguments to brief, quoted passages, creating an imbalanced presentation that subtly favors the anti-China stance.

title: Right Wing Upset That Funerals Last Week Were Political
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('snowflakes on the right are melting'), dismissive framing ('what did he do for a living?'), selective presentation of opposing views, and inflammatory characterizations without balanced context.

title: 'It's Hard to Watch': Hospital ERs Across The Nation Are Completely Overwhelmed With Non-'Covid' Patients 'Sicker Than They Have Ever Seen' - Nurses Forced to Give Treatments in Hallways Because No Beds are Available. Fake jab anyone?
justification: Strong bias with misleading framing and cherry-picked facts: the headline and article imply vaccines caused the ER surge without evidence, uses inflammatory language ('experimental vaccines,' 'Fake jab'), selectively omits plausible explanations (delayed medical care, pandemic backlog), and presents speculation as fact while suppressing legitimate counterarguments.

title: Facebook Testing 'A Number Of Approaches' To Limiting Political Content
justification: Nearly neutral reporting with straightforward attribution to Facebook officials and their announced policy; minimal editorial framing though the source (sgtreport) is known for conservative bias, the article itself uses objective language and reports facts directly from the company's statement.

title: Texas: Unaccompanied toddler and baby found abandoned near Rio Grande river
justification: Strong bias evident through selective framing (emphasizing abandoned children and Biden policy blame), charged language ('without remorse,' 'heartbreaking'), omission of context about Trump-era family separation policies, and one-sided presentation of the border crisis narrative that attributes causation to Biden's policies without balanced analysis.

title: Americans Overwhelmingly Want Russian Propaganda Banned
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing, charged language ('Putin faction,' 'propaganda bubble'), and editorial commentary that advocates for bans while dismissing opposing viewpoints; the article presents survey data but then adds interpretive analysis that favors one perspective without balanced counterargument.

title: Why Do Feds Keep Suppressing News Of Suspected Muslim Terrorists Crossing The Southern Border?
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('feds keep suppressing,' 'determined to cover it up'), selective presentation of facts (citing leaked Project Veritas video and removed CBP press release without providing full context), unsubstantiated claims about government conspiracies, and inflammatory framing that presents speculation as fact while omitting counterarguments or official explanations.

title: Trump's Inauguration Rabbi: Ignore Jimmy Carter On Israeli-Arab Conflict
justification: Article shows moderate bias through selective framing that emphasizes Rabbi Hier's criticism of Carter while providing limited context for Carter's perspective, uses charged language like 'wrong person,' and omits substantive counterarguments to Hier's claims about Hamas removal and Jerusalem's status.

title: CCP shamed by brave elder as he denounces the terrible treatment in strict lockdowns
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('shamed,' 'brave elder,' 'terrible treatment,' 'shameless, incompetent'), selective omission of context, and inflammatory framing that presents one-sided condemnation of CCP policies without balanced reporting of justifications or counterarguments; multiple copyright blocks obscure content verification.

title: California Catholic Conference drops opposition to bill that could legalize infanticide
justification: Strong bias through charged language ('one of the most extreme pro-abortion bills,' 'shockingly weak,' 'infanticide'), selective framing that emphasizes worst-case interpretations while omitting substantive counterarguments, and incomplete/corrupted text sections that prevent full verification of claims.

title: A Young Deer and the Forest Vila
justification: This is a literary text presenting a poetic ballad about a young deer and forest mythology, with scholarly annotations about comparative folklore across nations; it contains no news, claims, or bias as it is a historical literary work with footnoted references.

title: The real reason Donald Trump is doing this
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('what a crock,' 'shit-stirrer,' 'bilk'), dismissive framing of Trump's claims, and selective reporting that omits substantive counterarguments while relying on loaded characterizations rather than balanced analysis.

title: Supreme Court Blocks Title 42 From Ending
