justification: Strong bias evident through inflammatory language ('It's an invasion,' 'laissez faire approach,' 'unprecedented numbers'), selective presentation of facts without counterargument, and extensive self-promotional appeals that undermine journalistic credibility rather than reporting news.

title: Insurrectionist Josh Hawley Says Roe Protesting Democrats Are Anti-Democracy
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('insurrectionist,' 'incite,' 'coup,' 'enemy of democracy'), one-sided framing that dismisses Hawley's argument as 'double talk deflection,' selective editing of his quote with unexplained copyright blocks, and an editorial conclusion that his views 'should not be taken seriously' rather than presenting counterarguments.

title:
SOMEBODY SET UP US THE BOMB: Cargo Freeway Expansion Canceled in L.A. Due to 'Racist' Roads.
Th...
justification: Strong bias demonstrated through inflammatory language ('SOMEBODY SET UP US THE BOMB' headline), loaded framing ('diesel death zone'), sarcastic editorial commentary ('nice sneer'), cherry-picked facts omitting key context about the cancellation decision, and dismissive characterization of environmental/equity concerns as mere 'activism' without presenting substantive counterarguments.

title: Hey Patriots: The Communist Chinese Are Boosting The Lincoln Project ... Do You Care?
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('total dirtbags,' 'clowns'), selective facts, and misleading framing that conflates coverage with endorsement while omitting context about The Lincoln Project's actual funding and operations.

title: Constitutional Sheriff Calls For ALL of His Citizens to ALWAYS be Armed
justification: Subtle bias shown through selective framing that emphasizes the sheriff's pro-gun stance while omitting counterarguments about gun control or public safety concerns; the article focuses primarily on one perspective without balanced presentation of opposing views.

title: Why Mary Trump Is The Only Good Trump
justification: Strong bias evident through inflammatory framing ('only good Trump'), selective presentation of Mary Trump's critical statements without counterargument or opposing perspectives, loaded language ('criminal, cruel and traitorous'), and editorial voice asserting facts as opinions (e.g., 'as if he hasn't done enough already').

title: Fauci pushes back against ridiculous Republican attacks: 'How bizarre is that?'
justification: The article presents one perspective (Fauci's pushback) as the primary focus while describing Republican criticisms as 'ridiculous' and 'bizarre' in the headline and framing, with limited substantive engagement with Republican arguments or counterarguments beyond dismissive characterization.

title: Media bias exposed when first lady and Kamala Harris photographed wearing same boots
justification: Moderate bias evident through selective framing, cherry-picked headline comparisons, and one-sided presentation that favors the Republican perspective while omitting context about media coverage patterns or alternative explanations for different tones.

title: Leftist Riots Vs. Capitol 'Insurrections' And The Clueless Media That Conflates Them
justification: Strong bias through selective presentation of facts, charged language ('laughable mainstream press,' 'apoplectic,' 'gross display'), and dismissal of January 6 while emphasizing left-wing protests; presents disputed characterizations of events as fact without equivalent scrutiny.

title: The Everything Bubble Burst! Stocks, Housing And Bond Markets Will Collapse Simultaneously
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, alarmist language ('astronomical blowup,' 'monetary disaster'), and selective presentation of economic theory as inevitable fact without counterarguments or acknowledgment of alternative economic perspectives.

title: Michael Cohen and Michael Flynn caught meeting with alleged briber at Trump Tower
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('flunkies,' 'shady money,' 'criminal scandals'), speculative reasoning presented as fact (claiming if Flynn disclosed meeting to Mueller then 'Mueller has Cohen nailed for it'), selective framing of unverified claims, and omission of any counterarguments or alternative explanations for the meeting.

title: You don't say - what a bunch of idiots: Government departments ordered to STOP installing new Chinese-made security cameras and disconnect those already in use from their main computer systems over cyber 'security risks' linked to Beijing regime
justification: Strong bias evidenced by inflammatory headline calling government 'idiots', selective framing that emphasizes Chinese surveillance threats while omitting context about security measures or alternative camera sources, and cherry-picked facts (Hancock CCTV incident) designed to dramatize rather than inform.

title: Alabama, Arkansas, Iowa, Mississippi, Missouri, Montana, North Dakota, and South Carolina end unemployment!
justification: Article demonstrates subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis on Republican arguments against unemployment benefits without substantive counterarguments, while focusing primarily on the states ending programs rather than balanced presentation of both perspectives.

title: Biden's NSA Under Investigation for Illegally Spying on Tucker Carlson
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('hastily issued,' 'egregious surveillance'), selective framing that presents Carlson's claims as fact without adequate skepticism, omission of NSA's denial and counterarguments, and multiple copyright redactions that obscure sourcing and context.

title: You're A Lying, Dog-Faced One-Horse Pony Soldier
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy against Biden through inflammatory language ('Sleepy Joe,' 'crooked son'), misleading framing of a reported incident, crude personal attacks ('why his dentist ruined a perfect horse's ass'), and selective misrepresentation presented as analysis rather than fact.

title: I THINK ITS SIGNIFICANT THAT THIS IS IN THE WEEKLY STANDARD NeverTrumpers What if Trump really
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy for Trump and dismissive characterization of NeverTrumpers; uses charged framing ('afflicted with nagging suspicion'), cherry-picks positive economic outcomes while omitting counterarguments or criticisms, and presents Trump's policies as unambiguously successful without balanced analysis.

title: Anti-vaxxer Nurse who Jabbed 9,000 with Saline Instead of Covid Vaccine Spared Jail
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('anti-vaxxer' repeated in headline and opening, 'deadly virus,' 'shamed nurse') and selective framing that emphasizes the nurse's ideology while downplaying context; the article uses inflammatory tone and presents the narrative in a way that sensationalizes rather than objectively reports the incident.

title:
LIFTING LOCKDOWNS NOT THE CULPRIT BEHIND NEW SURGE IN CORONAVIRUS CASES, DOCTOR SAYS:
Then [Scott A...
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective quoting of Scott Atlas, characterizing protests as a primary culprit for case surges while dismissing lockdown concerns, and framing left-leaning officials' positions dismissively without presenting their full context or counterarguments from public health experts.

title: France: Accomplished Professor Thrown in Psych Hospital After Questioning Official COVID Narrative
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy: inflammatory framing ("silencing political opponents," comparison to Soviet repression), selective facts without counterargument, unverifiable claims about vaccine aluminum content ("17 times the maximum dose"), and promotion of conspiracy theories (lab-created virus, patent fabrication) presented as fact without substantiation.

title: HUGE OMNIBUS BILL INTRODUCED IN THE USA
justification: Moderate bias with selective focus on precious metals data and unsubstantiated claims (e.g., 'CRIME SCENE', 'HOUSE OF BRIBES') that lack supporting evidence; omits counterarguments and uses charged language while presenting fragmented, difficult-to-verify financial data.

title: Fox Reporter Asks McCain A 'Stupid' Question & Johnnie Instantly Goes Off The Rails
justification: Moderate bias: The article uses loaded language ('petty squabbles,' 'overgrown schoolyard bully,' 'stupid question') and frames McCain's perspective favorably while characterizing the president's motives negatively. The framing presents McCain's position as reasonable without substantive counterargument, and the characterization of Doocy's question as 'slanted' is asserted rather than demonstrated.

title: Jen Psaki Was Asked If Peter Doocy 'Sounds Like A Stupid Son Of A Bitch' Here's Her Response...
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('smeared,' 'liar,' 'unhinged'), selective presentation of facts, and dismissive treatment of opposing perspectives; multiple instances of opinion presented as fact and inflammatory characterizations of news networks and individuals.

title: Rudy Giuliani just made things even worse for Donald Trump
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy and charged language ('president' in quotes, 'brilliant suggestion' used sarcastically, 'sowing seeds of his own downfall'), selective presentation of facts favoring Biden/Democrats, dismissive framing of Trump's positions, and reliance on unnamed strategists and pollsters without rigorous sourcing.

title: COUP CONSPIRACY: FBI serves subpoenas on Pennsylvania Republicans
justification: Moderate bias evident through inflammatory headline ('COUP CONSPIRACY'), selective framing that emphasizes Republican wrongdoing while downplaying context, omission of substantive counterarguments, and charged language ('fake elector scheme,' 'illegally overturn') that presents disputed characterizations as established fact.

title: Portland Police Announce Manhunt for Marquise Love in Brutal Attack on Driver Pulled From Pickup by Antifa and Black Lives Matter Thugs
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('brutal attack,' 'thugs,' 'pulled him out'), unverified claims about 4Chan identification and Antifa/BLM involvement, selective framing that presents unconfirmed details as fact, and omission of police statements urging caution about social media misinformation while the article itself spreads potentially inaccurate narratives.

title: LifeSite staffer calls on young culture warriors to enlist in revamped internship program
justification: The article exhibits subtle bias through selective framing and promotional language ('highly-talented devout Christian fighting on the frontlines,' 'wonderful testimony') that favors the internship program without presenting counterarguments or critical perspectives, though it remains primarily factual in nature.

title: Inmate recognizes 70-year-old cellmate as kid sister's rapist, he's now serving 25 years for brutal vengeance
justification: Nearly neutral article with minimal bias; uses factual reporting of the incident, quotes from the inmate, and court documents, though the headline's framing of 'brutal vengeance' contains slight emotional language that subtly favors a sympathetic view of the perpetrator.

title: Podcast: The Three Whisky Happy Hour: Milk the Trump Dividend!
justification: Strong bias evident through loaded language ('COVID tyranny,' 'social science nonsense'), favorable framing of Trump with predictions of his cultural dominance, dismissive treatment of opposing perspectives, and the casual, partisan tone of a podcast celebrating Trump's political impact rather than analyzing it objectively.

title: We're about to get a big peek at what Merrick Garland really has in store for Donald Trump
justification: Moderate bias: The article uses speculative framing ('could provide clues,' 'it's anyone's guess,' 'no way of knowing') while repeatedly assuming Trump's guilt as a foregone conclusion ('preparing the public for Trump's imminent arrest,' 'about his guilt'), creating a presumption of culpability without presenting counterarguments or acknowledging the legal principle of innocence until proven guilty.

title: Donald Trump's ugly seven
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy and inflammatory language: 'traitor,' 'enemy of the state,' 'black as coal,' 'criminal on his way to prison,' and dehumanizing descriptions ('wears human skin') that misrepresent Trump's actions and cherry-pick facts to support a predetermined conclusion while dismissing any counterarguments.

title: Fund Led by Steyer and Soros Rolling Out State-Focused Donations Strategy
justification: Moderate bias: The article uses loaded framing ('liberal mega-donors,' 'leftwing pressure groups') and selectively presents financial data about donations without providing counterarguments or context from the organizations mentioned, while the heavy redaction makes full assessment difficult.

title: White House Coronavirus Team Quiet as Joe Biden Readies State of the Union Address
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis: the article highlights the coronavirus team's reduced visibility and characterizes CDC guidance changes skeptically (noting criteria shifts while implying inconsistency with prior "data" and "science" claims), while emphasizing Democratic political motivations for mask lifting without presenting counterarguments about the scientific basis for policy changes.

title: What to Make of Those UN Vehicles Parked in Ontario?
justification: Strong bias with selective facts and misleading framing: the article presents an elaborate conspiracy theory connecting UN vehicles, a pharmaceutical company, a unsolved murder, and vague insinuations without evidence, using charged language like 'creepy' and implying nefarious connections while omitting the straightforward explanation that Inkas manufactures armored vehicles for legitimate clients.

title: More Advanced Intelligent Life Could Exist Right Here On Earth
justification: Article exhibits moderate bias through selective use of credible sources, unverified claims presented as fact (e.g., Dedrickson's benevolent beings claim), speculative reasoning presented without adequate skepticism, and absence of counterarguments from skeptics or scientific establishment regarding UAP origins.

title: Donald Trump opens his mouth and destroys the legal argument for his own national emergency
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy: charged language ('racist base,' 'blabbering mouth,' 'rambling and narcissistic'), selective framing that presents Trump's statements as admissions of wrongdoing, and omission of counterarguments or Trump supporters' perspectives on the emergency declaration.

title: Ann Coulter: Are the Media Trying to Throw the Election to Trump?
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('worst of everything', 'utter spinelessness'), selective framing that dismisses media criticism while advocating for Trump, cherry-picked facts (the 'fine people' quote misrepresented without full context), and strategic omission of counterarguments; multiple copyright blocks obscure key claims making verification difficult.

title: WH Press Secretary Kayleigh McEnany's Press Briefing Preparedness Triggers Yet Another CNN Journalist
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy; uses charged language ('triggered,' 'smack downs,' 'laughable,' 'flat out laughable'), dismissive framing ('so-called reporters,' scare quotes around 'reporter' and 'fact checker'), selective presentation of facts omitting CNN's perspective, and inflammatory characterizations of journalists as unserious while championing McEnany without balanced analysis.

title: Randonautica = The Newest Psychological Operation
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy for a conspiracy theory narrative, selective use of facts, misleading framing (presenting unverified theories as established facts), and charged language ('PsyOp', 'dark web', 'Chaos Magicians') that cherry-picks connections to support the predetermined conclusion that Randonautica is a psychological operation.

title: Young Actress in Her 30's Suddenly Dies of 'Natural Causes'
justification: Strong bias with misleading framing and cherry-picked facts: article presents a tragic death as part of a conspiracy theory about 'excess deaths' without evidence, uses loaded language like 'suspicious' and 'silent crisis,' selectively omits context about the actual cause of death, and manipulates a personal tragedy to push an unfounded narrative about mysterious mortality patterns.

title: SpyCops: How the UK Police Infiltrated Over 1,000 Political Groups
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis: the article presents the 'Spycops' scandal primarily through a critical lens favoring activist perspectives, uses loaded language ('raped by the state,' 'enforcers for the upper class'), omits counterarguments about police justifications, and makes unsubstantiated comparative claims about differential treatment of far-right groups without evidence.

title: French General: People who resisted COVID jabs are 'superheroes' who 'embody the best of humanity'
justification: Strong bias with selective framing, charged language ('experimental COVID jabs,' 'draconian COVID policies'), and omission of scientific consensus on vaccine safety and efficacy; the article presents an unverified or misattributed letter as fact without adequate skepticism, and uses heroic language to frame vaccine hesitancy while dismissing mainstream medical guidance.

title: The Blessing of Brokenness
justification: Article is a personal testimony and religious reflection with no detectable bias; it presents the author's spiritual perspective in first-person narrative without making false claims about external facts or misrepresenting opposing viewpoints.

title: Donald Trump Mocks Biden Corruption: 'Hunter Has Inspired Me to Begin Painting'
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('crooked as hell'), selective framing that emphasizes only Trump's criticism while omitting context or counterarguments, cherry-picked details about Hunter Biden's art sales presented as evidence of corruption without substantiation, and sarcastic mockery presented as news narrative rather than balanced reporting.

title: Coordinated Attacks on AB5 Opponents Led by Law Professor Veena Dubal, Assisted by Twitter Employees
justification: Strong bias evident through charged language ('job-killing bill', 'professional agitator'), selective presentation of facts, unsubstantiated claims about coordination presented as established fact, and dismissive framing of harassment allegations without adequate evidence or counterargument from those accused.

title: We've all turned from normal humans into muzzled masochists! - Peter Hitchens
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('muzzles,' 'tinpot despot,' 'masochists'), inflammatory framing comparing pandemic measures to religious persecution, selective citation of evidence while dismissing contrary views, and overt advocacy against mask-wearing without balanced presentation of public health perspectives.

title: Dead Cat Receives Voter Registration Form by Mail
justification: Strong bias with selective facts and inflammatory framing: the article cherry-picks anecdotes about voter registration errors to suggest widespread fraud concerns, repeatedly names Stacey Abrams in a negative context unrelated to the main story, uses loaded language ('out-of-town activists'), and presents unverified claims about the Voter Participation Center's intent without adequate context or counterarguments.

title: The Great Reset Is Happening!
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy framing; the article presents protests as evidence of coordinated 'Great Reset' conspiracy without substantiation, uses loaded language ('knocking over the dominos toward total control'), selectively quotes commenters agreeing with the conspiracy narrative while omitting counterarguments or factual context, and presents conjecture ('Is it really a coincidence') as investigative discovery.

title: Nolte: Don't Forget Kamala and Team Biden Paid Bail for Rioters
justification: Strong bias with selective facts, inflammatory language ('terrorist group', 'anarchy', 'revolving door'), misleading framing that conflates bail fund donations with direct responsibility for riots, and omission of context that bail funds serve legitimate criminal justice reform purposes beyond the 2020 riots.

title: 3 Large Earthquakes And A Major Volcanic Eruption In Mexico Spark Fears That The Big One Could Hit California Soon
justification: Article exhibits moderate bias through selective emphasis on catastrophic scenarios and speculative fear-mongering ('spark fears,' 'nightmare beyond anything'), combined with vague causal connections between Mexico's seismic activity and California risks without substantive evidence; the numerous copyright symbols obscure content verification.

title: Fauci merges COVID & Climate: Infectious diseases 'largely the result' of human 'encroachment on nature' & 'often aided by climate changes' - Published in New England Journal of Medicine
justification: Strong bias: Article uses charged language ('monstrous,' 'bureaucrat'), cherry-picks quotes selectively (including sarcastic responses from critics like Berenson and Morano), presents conspiracy-adjacent framing ('keep both climate and COVID fears humming along'), and omits peer-reviewed scientific context while amplifying editorial commentary that dismisses scientific consensus.

title: Is French Article 24 a License for Police Brutality?
justification: Article shows subtle bias through selective framing and incomplete information: emphasizes protest violence details (window smashing, burned vehicles) while downplaying police response context, presents Michel Zechan incident as straightforward police brutality without full details, and cuts off abruptly mid-sentence suggesting editorial issues; however, core facts about Article 24, vote counts, and dates appear verifiable.

title: Joni Ernst Kisses Her Senate Seat Goodbye As She Refuses To Admit Trump's Failed Virus Leadership
justification: Strong bias demonstrated through charged headline ('Kisses Her Senate Seat Goodbye'), selective framing that emphasizes a perceived double standard without acknowledging Ernst's actual responses, and editorial commentary ('double standard been made more clear') inserted into what should be factual reporting.

title: NEW POLLS: Bernie Sanders is surging like gangbusters
justification: Moderate bias: The article uses charged language ('surging like gangbusters,' 'causing anxiety in Democrat establishment circles,' 'slap in the face to Warren') and selectively presents polling data favorable to Sanders while omitting context about Biden's performance or other candidates; the framing emphasizes Sanders' momentum without balanced perspective.

title: "You Have No Jurisdiction Over Me": US Citizen Shuts Down Arrest By Knowing the Law
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing that favors MacCormack's perspective; uses charged language like 'thwarting' and 'circumvent the constitution,' omits substantive counterarguments about immigration enforcement authority, and presents MacCormack's legal interpretation as fact without exploring competing legal perspectives or the officer's position.

title: #DELETED: 'Outspoken Trump Antagonists' Led Facebook 'Misinformation' Efforts
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy against Facebook and Big Tech through cherry-picked claims, unnamed sources, and inflammatory framing ('well was poisoned from the start'). Uses dehumanizing characterizations of employees as 'antagonists' and selectively presents unverified allegations without counterargument or Facebook's perspective.

title: I'm terrified that we are going to reopen our economy too quickly at the expense of human lives.
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy and charged language ('terrified,' 'dig hundreds of thousands of graves,' 'cannon fodder,' 'corrupt leaders'); presents only one perspective on reopening while dismissing economic concerns; uses emotional appeals and catastrophizing rather than balanced analysis of competing policy considerations.

title: Beijing prepares 4000-beds makeshift hospital for potential COVID outbreak
justification: Nearly neutral reporting with factual information about Beijing's COVID-19 preparations, though framing around 'potential outbreak' and reference to Shanghai as 'sign of the future' shows slight editorial slant; language is mostly objective despite some censorship artifacts in the text.

title: Texas Ends COVID Emergency Orders
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('lockdown nightmare,' 'entire scam,' 'phony statistical mirage'), unsupported claims about PCR testing and CDC manipulation, and selective presentation of facts while dismissing opposing evidence as meaningless.

title: Despicable TROLL mocks Gov. Abbott's faith, and he DROPS some THEOLOGY on him!
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('despicable troll,' 'scummy,' 'they don't care about truth'), one-sided framing that mocks critics while presenting Abbott's response as definitively superior, selective omission of the actual criticism, and editorializing ('Amen,' 'Incredible') that explicitly advocates for Abbott's religious perspective.

title: Middle School Student Suspended Over Drawings Depicting Guns Knives
justification: The article presents a one-sided perspective favoring the student and parent while dismissing school safety concerns; selective framing emphasizes the father's defense and questions the suspension's appropriateness without adequately representing the school's security protocols or explaining the specific policy violated.

title: European Elections - Read What You Want from These Statistics. Britain Cannot Decide on Brexit
justification: Moderate bias with selective framing: the article emphasizes Brexit Party's geographic dominance through maps while downplaying their actual vote share, juxtaposes petition signatures against Brexit Party votes to suggest media bias, and uses loaded language like 'rammed through' framing without presenting balanced counterarguments or alternative interpretations of the election results.

title: Report: McConnell Pushes Updated Version of 'Unconstitutional' Horse Racing Law in Spending Bill
justification: The article presents factual information about a real court ruling and legislative effort, but uses subtle framing choices like 'allegedly pushing' and 'purported purpose' that cast doubt, selectively emphasizes constitutional concerns without explaining the regulatory rationale or counterarguments for HISA, and ends abruptly suggesting incomplete reporting rather than balanced analysis.

title: One Day After Trump Pulls Out of Paris Climate Accord - Moscow Sees Snow in June
justification: Strong bias with misleading framing: uses charged language ('typical liberal fashion'), cherry-picks a single weather event to imply climate accord withdrawal is justified, omits scientific context that one snow event does not contradict climate trends, and presents an implied logical fallacy without evidence.

title: Video: MSNBC Worries Trump's Use Of The Word 'Sovereign' Is A "Dog Whistle"
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('king of fake news,' 'unabashed globalists'), selective framing that dismisses opposing viewpoints as a 'panel of leftists,' and misleading characterization of analytical discussion as conspiratorial ('dog whistle').

title: Trump Supporter Killed In Portland Over Weekend
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing, editorial commentary mixed with reporting, and emphasis on media bias narratives ('media has tended to ignore') while presenting one political perspective sympathetically and dismissing counterarguments.

title: Robert De Niro's Ultra-Luxury Restaurants Took Millions In COVID-19 Relief Money
justification: Strong bias shown through selective framing, charged language ('wealthy anti-Trump actor' repeated twice, 'embattled'), and rhetorical questions designed to inflame rather than inform; combines legitimate PPP loan facts with editorializing that conflates legal relief program participation with wrongdoing, while omitting context that many businesses received similar funding.

title: Despite false accusations, Liberty U ends school year with no coronavirus cases
justification: Strong bias through selective framing, inflammatory language ('attacked repeatedly,' 'false accusations,' 'false narrative'), one-sided presentation emphasizing only Liberty's claims without independent verification, and delegitimization of media coverage as 'one-sided' while presenting the university president's statements as fact without scrutiny.

title: Republicans demand release of secret report on Biden's 'open borders' immigration policies
justification: Strong bias evidenced by charged language ('liberal' repeated multiple times as pejorative, 'abuse executive authority'), selective framing that presents Republican claims as fact without verification, omission of DHS or Biden administration response, and characterization of media sharing as evidence of impropriety rather than normal reporting.

title: NEW Border Attack! ~150 Goblins Rush the Fence, Throw Rocks, Tear Gas is Back!
justification: Extreme bias with dehumanizing language ('goblins,' 'devil spawns,' 'brown children choking on tear gas' as celebration), inflammatory rhetoric, intentional misrepresentation of facts, racist slurs, and inflammatory framing designed to incite rather than inform.

title: Armed citizens, mass shootings, and "science"
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy against gun control research; uses charged language ('fiddled with the books,' 'perverted,' 'damaged people'), cherry-picks one critique to dismiss an entire field of research, and presents one researcher's opinion as definitive truth while dismissing opposing studies without substantive engagement.

title: Pastor Sues Governor Coonman After Being Fined and Jailed for Holding Peaceful Church Service
justification: Strong bias evident in inflammatory language ('Coonman' racial slur in headline, 'criminalizes religious worship'), selective presentation of facts favoring the pastor while omitting context about COVID-19 public health rationale, and framing that presents only one perspective on the constitutional question.

title: Gretchen Whitmer Could Face a Primary Challenge Next Year From a County Sheriff
justification: Strong bias evident through sarcastic framing ('with the wonderful job she has done so far, she is sure to be re-elected incredibly easily'), charged language ('made bigger potholes in all aspects of the state'), selective negative examples without counterargument, and transparent editorializing ('bourbon kicked in') that reveals the author's contempt rather than objective reporting of a potential primary challenge.

title: Eric Holder: Coronavirus 'An Opportunity' to Permanently Change America's Voting System
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing that emphasizes Holder's statements as admitting he 'saw coronavirus as an opportunity' while omitting context about election accessibility concerns, presenting his position as potentially self-serving, and using charged language like 'admitted' rather than neutral reporting; counterarguments about voting security are mentioned but dismissed rather than substantively addressed.

title: The dead giveaway that Michael Cohen is working on a plea deal against Donald Trump
justification: Moderate bias: The article employs speculative reasoning presented as investigative analysis, makes unverified claims about federal prosecutor tactics and Cohen's private communications, selectively emphasizes certain narratives while omitting counterarguments, and uses loaded framing ('dead giveaway,' 'speaks volumes') to guide reader conclusions about ongoing legal proceedings without presenting supporting evidence or opposing perspectives.

title: Declassify America's Dirty Secrets in Syria to Stop a Biden War
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('incorrigible,' 'con artists,' 'blathered'), selective cherry-picking of quotes to portray media and officials negatively, unsubstantiated claims about 'CIA-backed terrorists' and missing context on Syrian conflict complexities, and advocacy framing that presents one interpretive narrative as fact while dismissing counterarguments.

title: FNC's Napolitano Questions Trump's 'Fitness for Office' -- He Has 'Criminally Obstructed,' Disparaged Constitution
justification: Moderate bias with selective framing: the article presents Napolitano's critical commentary about Trump without counterargument or Trump's perspective, uses charged language ('criminally obstructed,' 'escaped prosecution'), and relies entirely on one viewpoint while omitting any defense or alternative interpretation of the actions described.

title: 'Top Chef' Star Padma Lakshmi, an Outspoken Biden Critic, Hosts 'Friends of Kamala' Fundraiser
justification: Moderate bias: The article selectively emphasizes Lakshmi's past Biden criticism to frame her current Harris endorsement as contradictory or insincere, while presenting her criticisms prominently and her support secondarily, creating an editorial narrative that questions her motives rather than neutrally reporting the fundraiser event.

title: Parnas Reveals Secret Ukraine Scheme Letter For Graham
justification: Moderate bias shown through charged language ('startling revelation,' 'corrupt self-interest,' 'shady business'), selective framing that emphasizes wrongdoing by Trump associates while omitting counterarguments or context, and reliance on a single source (Parnas's lawyer) without presenting alternative perspectives on the allegations.

title: Dr. Fauci Breaks Silence On Mail Voting After Sabotage Of USPS
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing that favors mail-in voting and portrays Trump's opposition as self-serving ('setting himself up to not have to admit defeat'), while emphasizing Fauci's support for mail-in voting and omitting substantive counterarguments to concerns about USPS capacity and election administration.

title: Liberal Canadian Prime Minister To License Media
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('dystopian', 'radical', 'dangerous'), selective framing that presents only one perspective, inflammatory comparisons to Orwell's 1984, unsubstantiated claims about Trudeau's actual policy positions, and omission of contextual details about what was actually proposed.

title: BREAKING: Iran Officially Tied To Hamas Conflict With Israel By Intercepted 'Suicide Drone'
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy and misleading framing: inflammatory language ('Biden...toying,' 'Mullahs...receive massive funding by American taxpayers to help pay for terrorism'), selective editing with multiple copyright symbols obscuring content, unsubstantiated claims about Biden's intentions, and cherry-picked quotes mixed with editorial commentary designed to support an anti-Iran, anti-Biden narrative.

title: Biden calls assault weapon ban the only rational option
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('outright fabrication,' 'nonsense,' 'ignoring reality'), selective presentation of Biden's statements without full context, one-sided advocacy for gun rights, and dismissive treatment of opposing viewpoints without fair representation.

title: White male National Guardsmen are an 'insider threat' says Democrat Rep. Steve Cohen
justification: Moderate bias with selective framing: the article emphasizes Cohen's controversial claims while highlighting that he provided no evidence, uses loaded language like 'accused' and 'raised eyebrows,' omits context about Cohen's actual statements, and includes a tangential anecdote about him liking a social media post to further delegitimize him, creating a narrative of Democratic irresponsibility rather than balanced reporting.

title: WHEN AUTHORITY VANISHES: Chicago's leaders have surrendered to vandals.
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy, charged language ('sacking,' 'hoodlums,' 'surrendered'), selective facts (dismissal rates without context), and one-sided framing that omits counterarguments or complexity regarding prosecutorial discretion, public safety priorities, or explanations from officials criticized.

title: Temple Mount Imam: 'We'll conquer the entire world and rule it through Islam'
justification: The article shows moderate bias through selective framing and loaded language ('they never stop,' 'free rein to incite violence'), while presenting primarily one perspective with minimal counterargument. The headline and framing emphasize the inflammatory quote without balanced context, and the article relies heavily on one advocacy organization's characterization of events.

title: Biden heads off to U.N. summit with climate agenda hanging in balance - Makes last-minute pitch for Build Back Better
justification: Subtle bias through selective framing and word choice: 'scrambled,' 'shaky ground,' 'empty-handed,' and 'defensive crouch' convey negative framing of Biden's position; the article emphasizes challenges and criticism while minimizing substantive discussion of climate policy details; multiple copyright redactions obscure full context of quotes.

title: Washington D.C. suspends Rudy Giuliani's law practice, but the case unlikely to be upheld
justification: Article contains subtle bias through selective framing and emphasis: describes courts' findings of 'false and misleading statements' as established fact while emphasizing Trump's defense and characterizing the suspension as 'unlikely to stand up,' creating an impression favorable to Giuliani despite presenting factual information about the disciplinary proceedings.

title: DC Mayor Baffles Residents By Lifting Mask Mandate for Only THESE People
justification: Moderate bias shown through sensationalized framing ('Baffles Residents'), selective emphasis on differential treatment ('for only THESE People'), and incomplete reporting that obscures key details while implying unfair policy-making without providing full context or counterarguments.

title: LAME! Biden Sucks Up to Radical Joy Reid: I Was Thinking of You as VP!
justification: Strong bias evident through charged language ('sucks up,' 'softball interview,' 'basement safe space,' 'swarmy sycophancy'), selective framing that portrays Biden negatively, dismissive characterization of Reid as having 'hateful rhetoric,' and editorial voice throughout that advocates against Biden rather than reporting neutrally on the interview.

title: VIDEO: Biden's Open Borders to Blame for Horrific Death of 46 Migrants Who Roasted to Death
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('roasted to death,' 'horrific,' 'Biden's deadly incompetence') and one-sided editorial framing that attributes the tragedy directly to Biden's immigration policies while omitting context about human smuggling networks, migrant desperation, and other contributing factors; presents political accusations as fact rather than opinion.

title: Donald Trump just tipped off that he thinks New York State is moving in on him
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing (emphasizing Trump's tweet as 'surreal' and 'tipping off'), speculative interpretation presented as fact ('the subpoena gives away that the grand jury is targeting Trump'), and editorial language ('mistakenly thought,' 'let's just say') that guides readers toward a predetermined conclusion about Trump's motivations and legal jeopardy.

title: Joe Biden's Higher Tax Dodge Is Being Assisted by the Media
justification: Strong bias with charged language ('higher tax dodge,' 'resort to semantics in order to sell a lie,' 'willfully avoiding'), selective presentation of facts favoring Trump's criticism, dismissal of Biden's position without balanced counterargument, and incomplete sourcing with multiple copyright blocks obscuring key claims.

title: Trump's Rothschild Bribe: Deutsche Bank Weighed Extending Trump Loans on Default Risk
justification: Strong bias through inflammatory headline ('Trump's Rothschild Bribe') that mischaracterizes the content, combined with selective framing that emphasizes reputational concerns as conspiratorial; the title invokes antisemitic tropes (Rothschild) unrelated to the article's actual reporting.

title: Morning Joe Paints Trump as a Sociopathic Dictator After Puerto Rico Visit
justification: Strong bias with overt advocacy against Morning Joe hosts through selective editing examples, charged language ('shamelessly,' 'misrepresenting'), and one-sided framing that dismisses the hosts' perspective while presenting Trump's comments as reasonably contextualized without substantive counterargument.

title: Selected Articles: Does the PCR Test Affect the Pineal Gland? Humans and "Transhumans." Dr. Astrid Stuckelberger
justification: Article contains extreme bias with unfounded conspiratorial claims (PCR tests affecting pineal gland), dehumanizing characterizations of political figures (Bennett described as favoring 'ethnic cleansing'), unverifiable accusations of media corruption and scientific suppression, fabricated narrative connections between unrelated topics, and inflammatory language designed to promote distrust in institutions and public health measures.

title: Trump administration sending tactical units to detain immigrants in sanctuary cities
justification: Moderate bias shown through selective framing, charged language ('elite units,' 'tactical units,' 'militarism'), and emphasis on Trump's anti-immigrant rhetoric while downplaying the stated security rationale; multiple copyright redactions obscure full context, and the article omits counterarguments about enforcement necessity.

title: Samantha Bee Still Doesnt Get It
justification: Strong bias through charged language ('geniuses' in scare quotes, 'blew it', 'Spoken like a true sexist'), selective framing that dismisses Bee's perspective, misrepresentation of her statements, and implicit threats ('Men have wives and daughters, and some might unfortunately find it an occasion for violence') combined with warning language ('Don't push us, Bee').

title: Mad Magazines Clout May Have Faded But Its Ethos Matters More than Ever Before
justification: Nearly neutral article with subtle framing favoring Mad Magazine's cultural importance; uses phrases like 'unadulterated truth-telling' and 'less credulous generation' that slightly favor the magazine's legacy, but maintains generally balanced discussion of its historical impact and present relevance.

title: Jerry Nadler: Antifa Violence In Portland Is "A Myth"
